exemplar.title: What role does editing play in the portrayal of psychological horror in Last Night in Soho and Black Swan, and how do these techniques enhance the audienceâs emotional response?

detailedExemplarData.title: What role does editing play in the portrayal of psychological horror in Last Night in Soho and Black Swan, and how do these techniques enhance the audienceâs emotional response?

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a clear and focused purpose throughout the investigation.
Research question is explicitly stated, well framed, and consistently linked to the analysis.
Methodology demonstrates an appropriate and varied range of relevant sources and methods with clear evidence of informed selection.
weaknesses: Minor digressions into broader genre history occasionally dilute the razor-sharp focus on editing techniques.
The coding process in the qualitative content analysis lacks detailed description of categories and reliability measures.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the studentâs introduction briefly detours into general horror history, the overall focus remains unwaveringly on editing within two psychological-horror films. The highly focused research question appears twice and guides every section, and the chosen qualitative methods (content analysis, theoretical lenses, interviews) are consistently appropriate. The few methodological gaps (e.g. more detail on coding) are minor in light of the coherent, systematic approach, justifying the top mark of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates excellent subject knowledge with accurate and consistent use of key terminology (e.g., âfragmented cuttingâ, âmatch cutâ, âdiegetic pacingâ).
Effectively applies theoryâformalist and psychoanalyticâto primary film examples, showing deep understanding.
Source material from primary texts and interviews is woven seamlessly into the argument to support technical discussion.
weaknesses: Overreliance on some popular web sources (blogs, Prezi) slightly undermines scholarly depth.
Certain technical terms (e.g. âmirror gagsâ) are introduced without precise definition or peer-reviewed citation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits a command of film-studies vocabulary and theoretical frameworks, consistently applying them to concrete filmic evidence. While some secondary sources lack academic rigour, the clear and sophisticated deployment of terminology and concepts across the analysis elevates the work into the top band, meriting the full 6 marks.

strengths: Research is extensive and consistently relevant, drawing on primary scenes, editor interviews, and appropriate theoretical texts.
Analysis is rigorous, linking editing techniques directly to psychological effects and supported by time-codes and production commentary.
Evaluation is critical, acknowledging methodological limitations and proposing further audience-based measures, demonstrating mature critical reflection.
weaknesses: Occasional leaps in reasoning (e.g. Freud connections) would benefit from additional empirical support.
Comparative claims regarding cultural background effects lack specific theoretical frameworks or examples to fully substantiate them.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation presents outstanding critical thinking: the research is deep and relevant, the analysis is well supported, and the evaluation section delivers a coherent, self-aware critique of methodology and scope. Minor gaps in counter-evidence and empirical linkage do not detract from an otherwise exemplary demonstration of critical thinking, justifying the maximum 12 marks.

strengths: Structure is impeccably organized with all required elements (title page, contents, headings, figure labels, bibliography) clearly presented.
Layout and formatting (pagination, consistent font, figure captions) are applied correctly and enhance readability.
weaknesses: A few minor layout glitches (empty cells in the contents table, inconsistent figure placement) remain.
Some caption formatting inconsistencies (font size, punctuation) should be standardized.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report meets and in places exceeds the EE presentation requirements: sections are clearly delineated, figures and tables are properly integrated, and formatting choices consistently support the argumentâs clarity. Minor typographical and layout inconsistencies are too limited to lower the top-band performance, warranting the full 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Pagination, font choice, and citation style are generally consistent, and images are captioned.  There are occasional line-break errors and spacing glitches, but layout considerations are substantially met.  This satisfies the descriptors for correct application within the 3-4 band; given the minor lapses, a score of 3 is appropriate.

The student clearly identifies the field (Film â psychological horror), frames the investigation around the craft element of editing, and consistently reiterates this focus throughout the introduction and later sections.  The purposeâto understand how editing enhances emotional response in two specific filmsâremains visible and appropriate for a systematic EE-level inquiry.  Minor digressions into broad genre history do not undermine the overall clarity, but they temper the impressiveness, so the strand is best located low in the top band (5).

Research draws on a respectable number of primary film scenes, production interviews, and contextual genre history, mostly tied back to the RQ.  Some sources are lightweight, but the majority remain relevant.  The breadth and pertinence fit the âgoodâ (7-9) range; limited scholarly depth keeps it at the lower edge, 7.

The methodology section outlines qualitative content analysis, lists primary texts, scripts, interviews, and theoretical lenses (formalist, psychoanalytic).  While not deeply critical of source reliability, the chosen methods are appropriate and varied, demonstrating informed selection.  Because the explanation of how data were coded is thin, the strand earns 5 rather than 6.

A coherent comparative argument runs through the essay and a dedicated Evaluation section notes limitations (sample size, audience data).  The critique is present but not deeply probing of counter-evidence or alternative readings, so it reaches the lower end of âgood discussion and partially critical evaluationâ.  A score of 7 is therefore fitting.

The essay includes the required title page, contents, clearly headed sections, consistent figure labelling, and bibliography, fulfilling the EE structural requirements.  Minor issues (some empty cells in the contents table, inconsistent figure placement) prevent perfection but the structure remains âclearly appropriateâ, meriting the top score of the 3-4 band.

Across the analysis the student demonstrates solid knowledge of film editing vocabulary and psychological-horror conventions, applying them to scene evidence.  Source use is generally relevant but leans on popular blogs, YouTube featurettes and secondary magazine articles; scholarly depth is uneven.  Hence the work meets the descriptors for clear but not âexcellentâ knowledgeâtop of the 3-4 band (4).

Scene analyses of mirror choreography, match cuts, and pacing link technique to psychological effect with logical explanation and visual evidence.  Assertions are usually supported by film time-codes or editor statements.  Some leaps (Freud linkage) are asserted rather than proved, so the work is âmostly supportedâ rather than exhaustively, earning an 8 mid-band score.

The research question is explicitly stated twice, is film-specific, comparative, and sharply delimited to âeditingâ within two named works.  Links to the forthcoming discussion (fragmented cutting, mirroring, pacing) are signposted, showing effective focus.  Slight breadth (two sub-questions implied) prevents a perfect 6, but the clarity and tight scope warrant a 5 in the highest band.

Terms such as âcross-cutâ, âmatch cutâ, âfragmented cuttingâ, âdiegetic pacingâ, and theoretical labels are used accurately and persistently.  Occasional phrasing slips (e.g., âmirror gagsâ without definition) are minor.  Consistency and correctness align with the top band; a mid-band 5 is appropriate given small inaccuracies.

annotations.comment: The title is clearly stated at the start, fulfilling the formatting requirement and immediately communicating the investigationâs focus on film editing.
The word count is provided and meets the 4000-word limit. This ensures adherence to IB formatting requirements.
The research question is well-defined but implies two sub-questions. Consider condensing it into a single, sharper inquiry to enhance focus and coherence.
The table of contents lacks page numbers, which impedes navigation. Populate all corresponding page references to improve structural clarity.
Citations like (LIMANTORO and ATMA) are incomplete. Include page numbers and full bibliographic details to strengthen source credibility.
Consider introducing the research question at the end of the introduction section to guide the reader more effectively into the analysis.
The research question is explicitly restated here and linked to investigative themes, reinforcing cohesion between introduction and analysis.
Connecting CGI mirror effects to psychological symbolism in Black Swan shows effective application of film theory to technical analysis.
The discussion of cross-cutting would benefit from clearer linkage to audience emotion data or theoretical frameworks rather than only descriptive effects.
Figure 4âs caption formatting is inconsistent (no hyphen before time codes and inconsistent font). Standardize caption style for clarity.
Clear signposting of the analysis scope (fragmented cutting, mirroring, disorienting transitions) provides a logical roadmap for the essay.
The qualitative content analysis is outlined, but the coding process is not detailed. Describe coding categories, reliability measures, and selection criteria to strengthen methodology.
Secondary sources like Freudâs work are referenced but lack critical engagement with their relevance. Critically justify why these theories apply to editing analysis.
The historical overview is extensive but risks diluting focus. Narrow the context to editing developments most directly relevant to the research question.
The inclusion of MathML tags (e.g., \sigma) appears to be an OCR artifact. Ensure all symbols and special characters are accurately formatted.
The description of psychoanalysis is repetitive and vague. Tighten the definition and connect it more explicitly to psychoanalytic film theory.
Figures are included but lack consistent in-text referencing (e.g., missing call-out for Figure 3). Ensure each figure is properly introduced and numbered in the narrative.
The analysis of mirror choreography is insightful and tied to specific time codes, demonstrating good integration of primary evidence.
The phrase âmirror gagsâ is used without definition. Define technical terms when first introduced to maintain academic precision.
Match cut analysis relies on StudioBinder blog definitions. Strengthen by referencing a peer-reviewed source to validate technical terminology.
The comparative synthesis of both filmsâ editing techniques is clear and helps to draw cohesive conclusions across case studies.
Figure 6 analysis could be enriched by quantifying pacing frequency (e.g., average shot length) to deepen empirical rigour.
Reliance on Prezi as a film techniques source weakens scholarly depth. Replace with peer-reviewed research or academic film analysis texts.
The bar scene montage analysis references Basedow without critique. Consider evaluating source reliability and supplement with academic studies on montage effects.
The discussion of subliminal perception is intriguing. Expand by linking specific editing sequences to empirical findings in cognitive psychology for stronger support.
The evaluation section acknowledges methodological limitations and proposes audience-based measures, showing good critical reflection.
The argument regarding cultural background effects is broad. Provide examples or theoretical frameworks to support this claim more robustly.
The bibliography contains many popular web and blog sources. To demonstrate academic rigour, integrate more peer-reviewed journal articles and authoritative film studies texts.
